Code 
library(vegabrite)
#lubridate if you need datetime variable instead of char for date

Exercise 1

data import and global transformation(if needed)

Code 
ex1_Data <- read.csv("swd-lets-practice-ex-2-3.csv") |>
  mutate(capacity = parse_number(capacity)) |>
  mutate(demand = parse_number(demand)) |>
  mutate(date = ym(date))

ex1_Data_unmet <- ex1_Data |>
  mutate(unmet_demand = demand - capacity)

ex1_Data_Long <- ex1_Data |>
  pivot_longer(!date, names_to = "data", values_to = "amount")

ex1Data_Long_Unmet <- ex1_Data_unmet |>
  select(date, unmet_demand, capacity) |>
  pivot_longer(!date, names_to = "what", values_to = "amount")

start on graphics

Code 
DCNextBar <- vl_chart() |>
  vl_add_data(ex1_Data_Long) |>
  vl_mark_bar() |>
  vl_encode_x(
    field = "date",
    type = "ordinal",
    timeUnit = "yearmonth",
    title = NA) |>
  vl_encode_y(
    field = "amount",
    type = "quantitative") |>
  vl_encode_color(field = "data",
                  sort = "descending") |>
  vl_encode_xOffset(field = "data",
                    sort = "descending") |>
  vl_legend_color(orient = "top",
                  title = NA) |>
  vl_add_properties(width = 300)
#DCNextBar
Code 
DCLine <- vl_chart() |>
  vl_add_data(ex1_Data_Long) |>
  vl_mark_line() |>
  vl_encode_x(
    field = "date",
    type = "temporal",
    title = NA) |>
  vl_encode_y(
    field = "amount",
    type = "quantitative",
    scale = list(zero = FALSE)) |>
  vl_encode_color(field = "data",
                  sort = "descending") |>
  vl_legend_color(orient = "right",
                  title = NA) |>
  vl_add_properties(width = 300)
#DCLine
Code 
DBar <- vl_chart() |>
  vl_add_data(ex1_Data) |>
  vl_mark_bar(size = 10, color = "red") |>
  vl_encode_x(
    field = "date",
    type = "ordinal",
    timeUnit = "yearmonth",
    title = NA) |>
  vl_encode_y(
    field = "demand",
    type = "quantitative")

CBar <- vl_chart() |>
  vl_add_data(ex1_Data) |>
  vl_mark_bar(color = "green") |>
  vl_encode_x(
    field = "date",
    type = "ordinal",
    timeUnit = "yearmonth",
    title = NA) |>
  vl_encode_y(
    field = "capacity",
    type = "quantitative")


DCLayerBar <- vl_layer(DBar, CBar) |>
  vl_add_properties(width = 300)
#DCLayerBar
Code 
#can not figure out how to switch order of stack
DUnDBar <- vl_chart() |>
  vl_add_data(ex1Data_Long_Unmet) |>
  vl_mark_bar() |>
  vl_encode_x(
    field = "date",
    type = "ordinal",
    timeUnit = "yearmonth",
    title = NA) |>
  vl_encode_y(
    field = "amount",
    type = "quantitative",
    stack = "zero") |>
  vl_encode_order(field = "what",
                  sort = "ascending") |>
  vl_encode_color(
    field = "what", 
    scale = list(range = c("green", "yellow", "red"),
                 domain = c("capacity", "unmet_demand", "demand")), 
    sort = c("capacity", "unmet_demand")) |>
  vl_add_properties(width = 300)

DUnDBar
Code 
DC_Point <- vl_chart() |>
  vl_mark_point() |>
  vl_add_data(ex1_Data_Long)|>
  vl_encode_x(field = "date",
              type = "ordinal",
              timeUnit = "yearmonth") |>
  vl_encode_y(field = "amount",
              type = "quantitative") |>
  vl_encode_color(field = "data",
                  sort = "descending")

DC_rule <-vl_chart() |>
  vl_mark_rule() |>
  vl_add_data(ex1_Data) |>
  vl_encode_x(field = "date",
              type = "ordinal",
              timeUnit = "yearmonth") |>
  vl_encode_y(field = "capacity",
              type = "quantitative") |>
  vl_encode_y2(field = "demand",
               type = "quantitative")
Warning: Invalid schema for object passed to or created by
modify_inner_spec.vegaspec_unit
Code 
DC_Complete <- vl_layer(DC_Point, DC_rule) |>
  vl_add_properties(width = 300)
#DC_Complete
Code 
unmet_demand <- vl_chart() |>
  vl_add_data(ex1_Data_unmet) |>
  vl_mark_line(point = TRUE) |>
  vl_encode_y(field = "unmet_demand",
              type = "quantitative") |>
  vl_encode_x(field = "date",
              type = "ordinal",
              timeUnit = "yearmonth") |>
  vl_add_properties(width = 300) |>
  vl_encode_color(value = "yellow")
#unmet_demand
Code 
#I know this is a roundabout way but I cant figure anything else out
row1 <- vl_hconcat(DCNextBar, DCLine)
row2 <- vl_hconcat(DCLayerBar, DUnDBar)
row3 <- vl_hconcat(DC_Complete, unmet_demand)
fullGraphic <- vl_vconcat(row1, row2, row3)
fullGraphic
Code 
DUnDBar

Exercise 2

a

Code 
jobs_url <- 
  "https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/vega-datasets@2.8.0/data/jobs.json"

perc_chart <- vl_chart() |>
  vl_add_data_url(jobs_url) |>
  vl_mark_bar() |>
  vl_encode_x(field = "year",
              type = "ordinal") |>
  vl_encode_y(field = "perc",
              type = "quantitative") |>
  vl_encode_color(field = "sex",
                  type = "nominal")

perc_chart

From this graphic we can tell that perc is the percent of jobs held in the dataset by sex and field. We can see this because for each year the perc amounts sum up of 1.00 or 100%

b

Code 
perc_chart_facet <- vl_chart() |>
  vl_add_data_url(jobs_url) |>
  vl_mark_point() |>
  vl_filter("datum.year == '1850' | datum.year == '1900' | datum.year == '1950' | datum.year == '2000'") |>
  vl_encode_x(field = "perc",
              type = "quantitative") |>
  vl_axis_x(format= ".0%") |>
  vl_encode_y(field = "job",
              type = "nominal",
              title = "") |>
  vl_encode_color(field = "sex",
                  type = "nominal") |>
  vl_facet(field = "year",
           type = "nominal",
           columns = 2) |>
  vl_add_properties(height = 500,
                    title = "Percent of Workers by Job in 50 Year Incriments")

perc_chart_facet

c

Code 
job_perc <- vl_chart() |>
  vl_add_data_url(jobs_url) |>
  vl_mark_point() |>
  vl_filter("datum.year == '1850' | datum.year == '1900' | datum.year == '1950' | datum.year == '2000'") |>
  vl_encode_x(field = "perc",
              type = "quantitative") |>
  vl_axis_x(format= ".0%") |>
  vl_encode_y(field = "job",
              type = "nominal",
              title = "") |>
  vl_encode_color(field = "sex",
                  type = "nominal") |>
  vl_facet(field = "year",
           type = "nominal",
           columns = 2) |>
  vl_add_properties(height = 500,
                    title = "Percent of Workers by Job in 50 Year Incriments")

job_perc

exercise 3

data wrangling

Code 
pump_price <- read.csv("pump_price_for_gasoline_us_per_liter.csv", check.names = FALSE)

CCodes <- read.csv("https://raw.githubusercontent.com/lukes/ISO-3166-Countries-with-Regional-Codes/master/all/all.csv")

long_pump <- pump_price |>
  mutate(across(`1991`:`2016`, as.numeric)) |>
  pivot_longer(
    cols = !country, 
    names_to = "year", 
    values_to = "price"
  )
Warning: There was 1 warning in `mutate()`.
ℹ In argument: `across(`1991`:`2016`, as.numeric)`.
Caused by warning:
! NAs introduced by coercion
Code 
long_pump <- long_pump |>
  inner_join(CCodes, by = c("country" = "name"))

graphic

Code 
gas_price_byRegion <- vl_chart() |>
  vl_calculate("datetime(datum.year, 0 , 1)", as = "year") |>
  vl_calculate("timeOffset('day', datum.year, 400 * random() - 200)", as = "jyear") |>
  vl_mark_point() |>
  vl_add_data(long_pump) |>
  vl_encode_x(field = "jyear",
              type = "temporal",
              title = "") |>
  vl_encode_xOffset() |>
  vl_encode_y(field = "price",
              type = "quantitative",
              title = "Price (USD$/Liter)") |>
  vl_encode_color(field = "sub.region",
                  type = "nominal",
                  title = "Sub Region") |>
  vl_add_properties(title = "Gas Prices by Global Sub-Region",
                    width = 500) |>
  vl_add_point_selection( 
    "sub_region_param", bind = "legend", encodings = list("color")) |>
  vl_encode_opacity(value = 0.2) |>
  vl_condition_opacity(param = "sub_region_param", value = 1)
gas_price_byRegion
